disallowing.definition: Not allowing; preventing someone from doing or feeling something.
disallowing.example: Disallowing a child's feelings causes harm.

devoted.example: They were devoted to each other and their son.

reputations.definition: What people think about you based on your past actions.
reputations.example: They worked hard at building up their reputations.

client.definition: A person who pays for a service.
client.example: They slowly built up their client bases.

bases.definition: Groups of regular customers or supporters.
bases.example: Their client bases grew over time.

flat.definition: An apartment; a home in a building with other homes.
flat.example: They bought a flat as an investment.

investment.definition: Something you buy hoping it will grow in value.
investment.example: The flat was an investment for their future.

insecure.example: Despite owning a flat, they still felt insecure.

settled.definition: Became comfortable and happy in a new place.
settled.example: Lucas started nursery but never settled.

employed.definition: Hired someone to work for pay.
employed.example: They employed a series of au pairs.

au pairs.definition: Young people from another country who live with a family and help with childcare.
au pairs.example: The au pairs would take Lucas to school.

series.definition: A number of things that come one after another.
series.example: They employed a series of au pairs over the years.

collect.definition: To go and pick someone up from a place.
collect.example: The au pair would collect him from school.

held.definition: In 'held in mind,' kept someone in your thoughts with care.
held.example: They always held Lucas in mind, even while working.

treat.definition: Something special done to make someone happy.
treat.example: They promised to take him for a treat at the weekend.

attempted.example: Aged ten, Lucas attempted to jump out of the window.

storeys.definition: Levels or floors of a building.
storeys.example: The flat was six storeys up.

prevented.example: He was prevented from jumping by his father.

alarming.example: This story is alarming to hear.

stress.definition: To say something is very important; to emphasize.
stress.example: I must stress that this is unusual.

distressed.example: They had no idea Lucas was so distressed.

gut.definition: A deep, natural feeling about something; instinct.
gut.example: He had a gut feeling that something must be wrong.

numbing.example: Numbing his feelings with medication did not seem right.

medication.definition: Medicine given to treat an illness.
medication.example: The doctor talked about anti-depressant medication.

anti-depressant.definition: Medicine used to treat depression and make someone feel less sad.

therapist.definition: A person trained to help people with emotional or mental problems.
therapist.example: Lucas went to see a therapist.

shipped.definition: Sent from one place to another, often without much thought.
shipped.example: Lucas felt like he was shipped about from place to place.

nuisance.definition: Something or someone that causes trouble or annoyance.
nuisance.example: Lucas felt like a nuisance because his care was hard to organize.

fond.definition: Having warm, loving feelings for someone.
fond.example: He would get fond of one au pair, only for them to leave.

replaced.definition: Put something or someone new in the place of the old one.
replaced.example: Each au pair was replaced by another.

contradict.definition: To say the opposite of what someone said.
contradict.example: His parents would flat-out contradict how he felt.

distract.definition: To take someone's attention away from something.
distract.example: They tried to distract him instead of listening.

cheer.example: They tried to cheer him up when he was sad.

unheard.example: Denying feelings makes children feel unheard.

console.definition: A game machine you play video games on.
console.example: He offered to buy Lucas a new games console.

gently.example: The therapist gently stopped him from arguing.

massive.example: They had a massive wake-up call.

wake-up call.definition: An event that makes you realize something important needs to change.
wake-up call.example: Lucas's crisis was a massive wake-up call.

cartoons.definition: Animated shows or movies, usually for children.
cartoons.example: They hung out and watched cartoons together.

loan.definition: Money you borrow and must pay back later.
loan.example: He got a loan so he could spend a month at home.

hung out.definition: Spent time together in a relaxed way.
hung out.example: They hung out, watched TV, and played games.

priority.definition: The most important thing that should come first.
priority.example: Lucas needs to feel he is the number-one priority.

guilt.definition: A feeling of having done something wrong.
guilt.example: Parental guilt does not help us or our children.

acknowledging.definition: Accepting that something is true or real.
acknowledging.example: Acknowledging our errors helps us change.

errors.example: We all make errors, but we can put them right.

ruptures.definition: Breaks or damage in a relationship.
ruptures.example: Ruptures in relationships can be repaired.

repaired.example: Problems only last if they are not repaired.

traditional.definition: Following old customs and ways of doing things.
traditional.example: Traditional gender roles made Annis feel more responsible.

gender.definition: Being male, female, or another identity.
gender.example: Traditional gender roles affected how she felt.

equally.definition: In the same way; to the same degree.
equally.example: Parents are equally responsible for their children.

generations.definition: Groups of people born around the same time; also, the passing of time from parent to child.
generations.example: It is hard to throw off the traditions of generations.

demonstrate.definition: To show or prove something clearly.
demonstrate.example: Demonstrate to your child that their feelings matter.

suicide.definition: The act of ending your own life.
suicide.example: Most children do not attempt suicide.

self-harm.example: Self-harm is a warning sign that something is wrong.

encourage.definition: To give someone support and confidence to do something.
encourage.example: Encourage your child to say how they feel.

delegate.definition: To give a task to someone else to do.
delegate.example: You cannot delegate love to someone else.

procrastinate.definition: To keep putting off something you should do now.
procrastinate.example: Do not procrastinate about giving love to your child.

psychiatrist.definition: A medical doctor who treats mental health problems.
psychiatrist.example: The child psychiatrist observed children at play.

psychoanalyst.definition: A person who helps others understand their deep, hidden feelings.
psychoanalyst.example: Winnicott was both a psychiatrist and a psychoanalyst.

disaster.definition: A very bad event that causes great harm.
disaster.example: Not being found and seen by anyone can lead to disaster.

deeds.example: Words only go so far; deeds go further.

injured.example: She was scared he might have badly injured himself.
